raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"'What do you think?' on a research excerpt most naturally invites evaluation of the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — specifically how sGMRES-T is positioned as a novel contribution to sketched Krylov methods.","R1.2":"The artifact chains a cited convergence theorem through a perturbation argument to a new composite bound; logical integrity of that derivation is a plausible load-bearing concern depending on review intent.","R1.3":"Empirical speedup claims (1.4–2.1×) and the heuristic l = ceil(log_2 k) recommendation are asserted without supporting detail; alignment between evidence and stated conclusions is plausibly central.","R1.4":"An explicit priority claim ('first sketched Krylov method with a field-of-values convergence guarantee') is made; assessing the delta vs. Nakatsukasa–Tropp and related work is likely expected under any reading of the delegation.","R1.7":"A specific theorem (Embree and Saad, 2019, Theorem 3.4) is cited by number and used as a load-bearing lemma; attribution accuracy and completeness of related-work coverage are plausibly in scope."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the review focus primarily on technical correctness (verifying the mathematical argument chain, citation accuracy, and evidence–claim alignment), or primarily on contribution framing (assessing the priority claim and positioning against prior art)?"}

rationale.R1.1: 'What do you think?' on a research excerpt most naturally invites evaluation of the thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim — specifically how sGMRES-T is positioned as a novel contribution to sketched Krylov methods.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains a cited convergence theorem through a perturbation argument to a new composite bound; logical integrity of that derivation is a plausible load-bearing concern depending on review intent.
rationale.R1.3: Empirical speedup claims (1.4–2.1×) and the heuristic l = ceil(log_2 k) recommendation are asserted without supporting detail; alignment between evidence and stated conclusions is plausibly central.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit priority claim ('first sketched Krylov method with a field-of-values convergence guarantee') is made; assessing the delta vs. Nakatsukasa–Tropp and related work is likely expected under any reading of the delegation.
rationale.R1.7: A specific theorem (Embree and Saad, 2019, Theorem 3.4) is cited by number and used as a load-bearing lemma; attribution accuracy and completeness of related-work coverage are plausibly in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus primarily on technical correctness (verifying the mathematical argument chain, citation accuracy, and evidence–claim alignment), or primarily on contribution framing (assessing the priority claim and positioning against prior art)?
